questions.question: Who is the head of the State Government in India?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the constitutional head of each State in India, appointed by the President.

questions.question: How long is the term of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India serves a 5-year term and can be re-elected.

questions.question: Under which article can the President proclaim a National Emergency?
questions.explanation: Article 352 empowers the President to proclaim a National Emergency if the security of India is threatened by war, external aggression, or armed rebellion.

questions.question: Under which article can the President impose President's Rule in a State?
questions.explanation: Article 356 (President's Rule) is invoked when the constitutional machinery of a State fails. It is also called State Emergency.

questions.question: The Governor of a state is appointed by:
questions.explanation: The Governor is appointed by the President of India (Article 155). The Governor is the constitutional head of the state (like President at national level) and acts as the agent/representative of the Centre in the state.

questions.question: The President of India can be impeached for:
questions.explanation: The President can be impeached only for violation of the Constitution (Article 61). The process starts in either House (special majority of 2/3rd) and is ratified by the other House.

questions.question: The 'Kitchen Cabinet' refers to:
options: Informal group of influential advisors to PM
questions.explanation: Kitchen Cabinet is an informal term for a small group of close advisors (not necessarily ministers) who the PM consults informally on important decisions, bypassing the formal Cabinet.

questions.question: The 'Union Cabinet' is different from 'Council of Ministers' in that:
options: Union Cabinet is a smaller body of senior Cabinet Ministers
Council of Ministers has only PM and senior ministers
questions.explanation: Council of Ministers includes Cabinet Ministers, Ministers of State (independent charge), and Ministers of State (no independent charge). Cabinet is a smaller sub-set of senior ministers who collectively decide policy.

questions.question: The Governor's rule in a state (Article 356) was first imposed in:
questions.explanation: Article 356 (President's Rule) was first imposed in Punjab in 1951. It has been imposed over 100 times since independence — Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh have had it most often.

questions.question: The Attorney General of India is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Article 76: Attorney General is appointed by the President (on advice of the Council of Ministers). The AG must be qualified to be a judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Central Information Commission (CIC) was established under:
questions.explanation: Central Information Commission was established under the Right to Information Act 2005 to hear appeals and complaints related to public information. Chief Information Commissioner and commissioners are appointed by President.

questions.question: The Inter-State Council is chaired by:
questions.explanation: The Inter-State Council (Article 263) is chaired by the Prime Minister. It consists of Chief Ministers of all states and 6 Cabinet Ministers of the Union as members.

questions.question: Article 356 (President's Rule) has never been used in:
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has had President's Rule imposed several times but not after 1980. This is a debatable question — various sources differ on exact records.

questions.question: Who administers the oath of office to the Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: The President administers the oath of office to the Vice President (Art 69). Whereas, the Chief Justice of India administers the oath to the President.

questions.question: Under which Article is the Prime Minister appointed?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President. Article 74 states the Council of Ministers with the PM at head shall aid and advise the President.

questions.question: Which case established that the President must act on the advice of the Council of Ministers?
options: Shamsher Singh v. State of Punjab (1974)
questions.explanation: Shamsher Singh v. State of Punjab (1974) — the Supreme Court held that the President and Governors are constitutional heads and must act on the advice of the Cabinet/Council of Ministers.

questions.question: A Judge of a High Court can be transferred by:
options: President on recommendation of CJI after consultation with collegium
questions.explanation: A Judge of a High Court can be transferred by the President after consultation with the Chief Justice of India (Art 222). The collegium system now governs such transfers.

questions.question: The Governor of a State holds office:
options: During the pleasure of the President
For life tenure like a judge
questions.explanation: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President (Art 156). Though the normal term is 5 years, the President (on advice of Cabinet) can remove the Governor at any time.

questions.question: Under Article 163, the Governor can act in his discretion regarding:
options: Certain specific matters listed in the Constitution
None — Governor must always act on CM's advice
questions.explanation: Article 163 states Governor acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers except when the Constitution expressly requires him to act in his discretion (like sending report for President's Rule under Art 356).

questions.question: The Finance Commission of India is constituted under:
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission is constituted under Article 280 by the President every fifth year (or earlier). It recommends distribution of taxes between Centre and States and grants-in-aid to States.

questions.question: The 'Doctrine of Pleasure' in the Indian Constitution means:
options: Certain officers hold office at the pleasure of the President/Governor
Citizens have the pleasure of removing officials
Courts can remove officials at pleasure
Parliament can dismiss officers at any time
questions.explanation: The Doctrine of Pleasure (Art 310) means certain government servants hold office during the pleasure of the President or Governor. However, Art 311 protects civil servants from arbitrary dismissal — they must be given a hearing.

questions.question: Which Article provides for special provisions relating to language spoken by a section of a State's population?
questions.explanation: Article 347 states the President may, on demand, direct that a language spoken by a substantial proportion of the State's population be officially recognized for that State. Punjabi in Haryana is an example.

questions.question: Under Article 72, the President's pardoning power includes:
options: All except offences under State laws
questions.explanation: Article 72 gives the President power to grant pardon, reprieve, respite, remission of punishment or commute the sentence — in cases of court martial, offences against Union laws, and death sentences.

questions.question: The power of the Governor to pardon under Article 161 differs from Art 72 (President) in that the Governor:
options: Can pardon all types of offences
Has more powers than the President
questions.explanation: Under Art 161, the Governor can pardon, reprieve, respite, remit, or commute sentences for offences against State laws. But unlike the President, the Governor CANNOT pardon death sentences — only the President can under Art 72.

questions.question: The Constitution of Jammu & Kashmir was abrogated by which action?
options: Presidential Order abrogating Article 370 in August 2019
J&K Reorganization Act 2019 replacing the State with two UTs
Both Presidential Order (abrogating Art 370) and J&K Reorganization Act 2019
questions.explanation: Article 370 was abrogated by Presidential Order on 5 August 2019, and simultaneously the J&K Reorganization Act 2019 bifurcated the State into two Union Territories — J&K (with legislature) and Ladakh (without legislature).

questions.question: The Constitutional provision for special officer for Linguistic Minorities is under:
questions.explanation: Article 350B provides for a Special Officer for Linguistic Minorities to investigate all matters relating to safeguards for linguistic minorities. The officer reports to the President annually.

questions.question: The National Development Council (NDC) is chaired by:
questions.explanation: The National Development Council is chaired by the Prime Minister. It includes all Union Cabinet Ministers, Chief Ministers of all States, Lt. Governors/Administrators of UTs, and members of NITI Aayog.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the composition of the UPSC?
questions.explanation: Article 316 deals with the appointment and term of office of UPSC members. The Chairman and members are appointed by the President. They can serve for 6 years or until age 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Which article gives the President the power to grant pardon?
questions.explanation: Article 72 empowers the President to grant pardon, reprieve, respite, or remission of punishment to persons convicted of offences.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India (1950-1962), serving for two consecutive terms.

questions.question: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru was India's first Prime Minister, serving from 15 August 1947 until his death on 27 May 1964.

questions.question: The Finance Commission of India is constituted by:
options: President of India every 5 years (Article 280) to recommend how taxes are divided between Centre and States
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission (Article 280) is constituted by the President every 5 years. It recommends: how central taxes are shared with states, grants-in-aid to states, and any other matter referred. The 15th Finance Commission (2021-26) recommended 41% tax devolution to states.

questions.question: Who administers the oath to the President?
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India administers the oath to the President (Article 60). In CJI's absence, the senior-most judge of the Supreme Court administers it.

questions.question: The President acts on the aid and advice of:
options: Council of Ministers headed by PM
questions.explanation: Article 74: Council of Ministers with PM as head shall aid and advise the President, who shall act in accordance with such advice (after 42nd and 44th Amendments, this is binding).

questions.question: The Finance Commission is constituted under:
questions.explanation: Article 280: Finance Commission is constituted by the President every 5 years to recommend distribution of tax revenues between Centre and States and grants-in-aid to states.

questions.question: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) is established by:
questions.explanation: Article 315 establishes the UPSC. It conducts examinations for appointments to the services of the Union. Its chairman is appointed by the President.

questions.question: The National SC/ST Commission is chaired by:
options: A member from SC/ST community appointed by President
questions.explanation: National Commission for Scheduled Castes (Art. 338) and SC/ST has a Chairperson, Vice-Chairperson and other members appointed by the President.

questions.question: The Chairman of NITI Aayog is:
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the ex-officio Chairman of NITI Aayog. It also has a CEO (appointed by PM), Vice Chairman, and Governing Council (all CMs and LGs of states/UTs).

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution talks about abolition of titles?
questions.explanation: Article 18 abolishes titles: No title shall be conferred by the State. Citizens cannot accept titles from foreign governments without President's consent. Exceptions: Military/academic distinctions.

questions.question: The Inter-State Council (Art 263) is chaired by:
questions.explanation: The Inter-State Council is chaired by the Prime Minister and includes Chief Ministers of all States and Administrators of UTs with legislatures, plus 6 Cabinet Ministers nominated by PM.

questions.question: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) is a:
questions.explanation: UPSC is a Constitutional body established under Article 315. It recruits civil servants for All India Services and Central Services. Its Chairperson can be removed only by Presidential order.

questions.question: The National Commission for Backward Classes was given constitutional status by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 102nd Amendment (2018) gave constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes under Art 338B. It also inserted Art 342A empowering President to specify Other Backward Classes.

questions.question: The Governor of Tamil Nadu is appointed by:
questions.explanation: The Governor of a State is appointed by the President of India under Art 155. By convention, this is done on the advice of the Union Cabinet (PM). The Governor holds office during the President's pleasure.

questions.question: Union Territories are administered by:
questions.explanation: Union Territories are administered by the President of India (Article 239) through a Lieutenant Governor (LG) or Administrator appointed by the President.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Vice President of India is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are covered in Part IV of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The President of India can nominate how many members to the Lok Sabha from the Anglo-Indian community?
questions.explanation: The President nominates two members from the Anglo-Indian community to Lok Sabha if the community is not adequately represented.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 gives the President the power to promulgate ordinances during recess of Parliament.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President. Under which article is this provision?
questions.explanation: Article 75 states that the Prime Minister is appointed by the President and other ministers on PM's advice.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under Part III of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property is a legal right under Article 300A, not a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) deals with Panchayati Raj Institutions in rural areas.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the Governor is appointed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state, including Tamil Nadu, is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution lists the subjects under the Union, State, and Concurrent Lists?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule contains the three lists that define the division of powers between Union and State.

questions.question: Under which article of the Indian Constitution is the Prime Minister appointed by the President?
questions.explanation: Article 75 deals with the appointment of the Prime Minister and other ministers by the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a qualification for election as President of India?
options: Must be a citizen of India
Must have completed 35 years of age
Must be a member of either House of Parliament
Must be qualified to be a member of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The President cannot be a member of Parliament or State Legislature; the other three are valid qualifications under Article 58.

questions.question: Who administers the oath of office to the President of India?
questions.explanation: Under Article 60, the Chief Justice of India administers the oath to the President.

questions.question: The President of India exercises the power to grant pardons under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 empowers the President to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment.

questions.question: The electoral college for the election of the President of India consists of elected members of:
options: Both Houses of Parliament and Legislative Assemblies of States
Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha and Legislative Councils
questions.explanation: Article 54 specifies the electoral college includes elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of Legislative Assemblies of States.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is the head of the:
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers, which aids and advises the President under Article 74.

questions.question: The President can promulgate ordinances when:
options: Both Houses of Parliament are in session
questions.explanation: Under Article 123, the President can promulgate ordinances when both Houses of Parliament are not in session.

questions.question: The salary and allowances of the President of India are determined by:
questions.explanation: Article 59 provides that the President's emoluments and allowances are determined by Parliament by law.

questions.question: In case of a vacancy in the office of the President, who acts as President?
questions.explanation: Article 65 states that the Vice President acts as President when the office is vacant.

questions.question: The Governor of Tamil Nadu is appointed by the:
questions.explanation: Under Article 155, the Governor of a state is appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.

questions.question: Who has the power to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 of the Constitution empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Under Article 75, the President appoints the Prime Minister, who is the leader of the majority party in Lok Sabha.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become the President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 58 of the Constitution sets the minimum age for the President at 35 years.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Duties?
questions.explanation: Article 51A, added by the 42nd Amendment, lists the Fundamental Duties of citizens.

questions.question: How many schedules are there in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution originally had 8 schedules; currently there are 12 schedules after amendments.

questions.question: Who has the power to dissolve the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The President dissolves the Lok Sabha on the advice of the Prime Minister under Article 85.

questions.question: Which legislation governs the Panchayati Raj system in Tamil Nadu?
options: Both 73rd Amendment and Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment provides the framework, and Tamil Nadu enacted its own Panchayats Act in 1994 to implement it.

questions.question: Who can remove a judge of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: A Supreme Court judge can be removed only by an impeachment motion passed by both Houses of Parliament.

questions.question: Which article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on these grounds, while Article 14 guarantees equality before law.

questions.question: The President of India can be removed from office by?
questions.explanation: Impeachment under Article 61 is the process for removal of the President.

questions.question: Which article empowers the President to promulgate ordinances?
questions.explanation: Article 123 gives the President ordinance-making power when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is the head of?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers and the government.

questions.question: The President's rule in a state is imposed under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 356 allows President's rule in states on failure of constitutional machinery.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is the ex-officio chairperson of?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister chairs NITI Aayog, which replaced the Planning Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a power of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Declaring war is a power of Parliament, not the President individually.

questions.question: The minimum age for election as President of India is?
questions.explanation: Article 58 requires the President to be at least 35 years old.

questions.question: The Prime Minister submits the resignation of the Council of Ministers to?
questions.explanation: Under Article 75, the Council of Ministers resigns to the President.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution can the President of India promulgate an ordinance when both Houses of Parliament are not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during recess of Parliament.

questions.question: The impeachment of the President of India can be initiated only on the grounds of:
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which authority has the power to remove the Prime Minister from office?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister holds office during the pleasure of the President but is removed if Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion.

questions.question: The power of the President to grant pardons is derived from which article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 confers on the President the power to grant pardons, reprieves, etc. in certain cases.

questions.question: In the Indian parliamentary system, the Prime Minister is the head of:
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers and aids and advises the President.

questions.question: During a national emergency declared under Article 352, the President acts on the advice of:
questions.explanation: The President can proclaim emergency only on the written advice of the Prime Minister and the Cabinet.

questions.question: Who among the following appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: As per Article 75 of the Constitution, the Prime Minister is appointed by the President.

questions.question: What is the maximum period for which the President can withhold assent to a Money Bill passed by Parliament?
options: No such period; President must give assent
questions.explanation: The President cannot return a Money Bill for reconsideration; he must either give or withhold assent, but withholding is not time-bound.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the President to seek information from the Prime Minister regarding the administration of Union affairs?
questions.explanation: Article 78 lays down the duties of the Prime Minister, including furnishing information to the President as requested.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution of India is the office of the President established?
questions.explanation: Article 52 provides for the President of India. Article 53 deals with executive power, Article 54 with election, and Article 55 with manner of election.

questions.question: Who administers the oath of office to the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: The President administers the oath of office to the Prime Minister and other ministers as per Article 75.

questions.question: The President of India can be removed from office by impeachment for violation of the Constitution. Which article governs this process?
questions.explanation: Article 61 lays down the procedure for impeachment of the President. Articles 62-64 deal with other matters.

questions.question: In the election of the President of India, the value of the vote of an MLA is determined by the formula: Total population of the state divided by the total number of elected members of the Legislative Assembly, further divided by 1000. This formula is based on which principle?
options: Parity between states and the Union
questions.explanation: The formula ensures parity between states and the Union by giving equal weight to votes of MLAs and MPs.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President. Which of the following is a constitutional requirement for the appointment?
options: The Prime Minister must be a member of Lok Sabha
The Prime Minister must be a member of either House of Parliament
The Prime Minister must be a citizen of India by birth
The Prime Minister must have prior ministerial experience
questions.explanation: Article 75 only requires that the Prime Minister be a member of either House of Parliament. Citizenship by birth is not mandatory.

questions.question: If the President of India is unable to discharge functions due to absence, illness, or any other cause, who acts as President?
questions.explanation: The Vice President acts as President in case of vacancy or inability, as per Article 65.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the relationship between the President and the Prime Minister is correct?
options: The President is the head of the state and the Prime Minister is the head of the government
The President can dismiss the Prime Minister at will
The Prime Minister is appointed by the President without any advice
The President exercises all executive powers directly
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional head, while the Prime Minister is the real executive head leading the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: The President of India has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment. This power is exercised under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 confers the pardoning power on the President. Article 73 deals with executive power, Article 74 with Council of Ministers, and Article 75 with appointment of ministers.

questions.question: Which of the following is a qualification for election as President of India?
options: Must be a member of Parliament
Must have completed the age of 35 years
Must be a graduate from a recognized university
Must have held a high judicial office for at least 5 years
questions.explanation: Article 58 requires the President to be a citizen of India, have completed 35 years of age, and be qualified for election as a member of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution of India is the office of the President of India established?
questions.explanation: Article 52 provides for the President of India, while Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is the head of which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers, as per Article 74, and aids and advises the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is a discretionary power of the President of India?
options: Appointing the Prime Minister when no party has a clear majority
questions.explanation: The President can exercise discretion in appointing a Prime Minister when no single party secures a majority in Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The President of India can be removed from office through which process?
options: Vote of no confidence by Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution, requiring a special majority in both Houses.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, which of the following is a key feature of the Panchayati Raj system as per the 73rd Amendment?
options: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
Direct election of the Prime Minister
Appointment of District Collectors by the President
Control of Panchayats by the State Legislature
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandates reservation of seats for SCs and STs in proportion to their population in Panchayats.

questions.question: The President of India has the power to grant pardons under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 empowers the President to grant pardons, reprieves, or remissions of punishment in certain cases.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Prime Minister of India is correct?
options: The Prime Minister is the ex-officio chairperson of the NITI Aayog
The Prime Minister is elected directly by the people
The Prime Minister serves a fixed term of five years
The Prime Minister can be removed by the President without Parliament's approval
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the ex-officio chairperson of the NITI Aayog, as per its governing council structure.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution vests the executive power of the Union in the President?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President, while Article 52 provides for the office of President.

questions.question: Under which article is the Prime Minister appointed by the President?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister is appointed by the President, and other ministers on advice of PM.

questions.question: The President can promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances having the force of law when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: Who recommends the imposition of President's Rule in a state under Article 356?
questions.explanation: The Governor submits a report to the President recommending President's Rule, based on which the President can issue a proclamation.

questions.question: In the event of a vacancy in the office of the President, who acts as President?
questions.explanation: Under Article 65, the Vice-President acts as President when there is a vacancy due to death, resignation, or removal.

questions.question: The President's power to grant pardons under Article 72 is exercised on the advice of which body?
questions.explanation: The President exercises all powers, including pardons, on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers as per Article 74.

questions.question: To whom does the Prime Minister submit the resignation of the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Under Article 75(2), ministers hold office during the pleasure of the President, so resignation is submitted to the President.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is the head of which of the following?
options: Both Council of Ministers and Cabinet
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers as per Article 74 and also the head of the Cabinet.

questions.question: Who elects the President of India?
options: Elected by members of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Elected by elected members of Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha and Legislative Assemblies of States
Elected by members of Parliament and State Legislatures
Elected by electoral college including nominated members
questions.explanation: Article 54 provides that the President is elected by an electoral college consisting of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the Legislative Assemblies of States, excluding nominated members.

questions.question: What is the term of office of the President of India?
options: Five years from the date of election
Five years from the date of assuming office
Five years from the date of notification
Not fixed, depends on President's discretion
questions.explanation: Article 56 states that the President holds office for a term of five years from the date on which he enters upon his office.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes the roles of President and Prime Minister?
options: President is the head of the state and PM is the head of the government
President is both head of state and government
President is ceremonial, PM is head of state
questions.explanation: In the Indian parliamentary system, the President is the constitutional head of state, while the Prime Minister is the real executive head of government.

questions.question: What majority is required in Parliament to impeach the President?
options: Two-thirds majority of the total membership of each House
Two-thirds majority of Lok Sabha only
Majority of total membership of both Houses
questions.explanation: Impeachment of the President requires a resolution passed by a two-thirds majority of the total membership of each House of Parliament.

questions.question: When does the President address both Houses of Parliament?
options: At the first session after each general election and at the start of the first session each year
Only at the start of the budget session
questions.explanation: Article 87 requires the President to address both Houses at the first session after each general election and at the commencement of the first session of each year.

questions.question: Who accepts the resignation of the Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The President accepts the resignation of the Prime Minister as the constitutional head of the executive.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 58 specifies that a person must be at least 35 years of age to be eligible for election as President.

questions.question: Who administers the Contingency Fund of India?
questions.explanation: Under Article 267, the Contingency Fund of India is placed at the disposal of the President to meet urgent unforeseen expenditure.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution provides for the election of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 54 deals with the election of the President; Article 52 establishes the office of the President.

questions.question: The President of India can be removed by impeachment for violation of the Constitution. Which article governs this process?
questions.explanation: Article 61 lays down the procedure for impeachment of the President.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President. Which article of the Constitution deals with this?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President.

questions.question: If the President of India resigns, to whom is the resignation addressed?
questions.explanation: As per Article 56, the President's resignation is addressed to the Vice President.

questions.question: The Prime Minister can be a member of which of the following houses?
options: Either Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha
Neither, as he is appointed by President
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister can be from either House of Parliament; e.g., Indira Gandhi was in Rajya Sabha when first appointed.

questions.question: The President has the power to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session. Under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 grants ordinance-making power to the President; Article 213 is for the Governor.

questions.question: In the exercise of executive power, the President acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers. Which constitutional amendment made this advice binding?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) made the advice of the Council of Ministers binding on the President.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the relationship between the President and the Prime Minister?
options: President is the head of the state; Prime Minister is head of the government
President is head of government; Prime Minister is head of state
Prime Minister is subordinate to President
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional (nominal) head of state, while the Prime Minister is the real executive head of government.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can the President of India promulgate an ordinance when both Houses of Parliament are not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during the recess of Parliament.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President. Which of the following qualifications is NOT required for a person to be appointed as Prime Minister?
options: Must be a citizen of India
Must be a member of either House of Parliament
Must have completed 30 years of age if in Rajya Sabha
Must be a member of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister can be from either Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha; membership of Lok Sabha is not mandatory.

questions.question: In the context of the Preamble of the Indian Constitution, which word was added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles is specifically based on the Gandhian philosophy?
options: Equal pay for equal work for both men and women
Right to work, to education and to public assistance
questions.explanation: Article 48 prohibits slaughter of cows and calves, reflecting Gandhian ideals.

questions.question: As per the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, which of the following is the minimum age required for a person to be elected as a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: Article 243F prescribes the minimum age of 21 years for membership in a Panchayat.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced the concept of 'Anti-Defection Law' in India?
questions.explanation: The 52nd Amendment (1985) added the Tenth Schedule to the Constitution dealing with anti-defection.

questions.question: The Governor of a State is appointed by the President of India. Who administers the oath of office to the Governor?
options: Chief Justice of the High Court
questions.explanation: The oath is administered by the Chief Justice of the concerned High Court, or in his absence by the senior-most judge.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens and not to foreigners?
options: Right to freedom of speech and expression
Right to life and personal liberty
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, etc.) is guaranteed only to citizens of India.

questions.question: In the election of the President of India, the value of the vote of a Member of Parliament is determined by?
options: Total population of India divided by total number of elected MPs
Total population of India divided by total number of MLAs
Total number of elected MLAs multiplied by 100
Total number of votes of Legislative Assemblies divided by total MPs
questions.explanation: The value of an MP's vote is derived by dividing the total population of India by the total number of elected MPs (Article 55).

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu state policy/act established the 'District Planning Committees' as per the 74th Amendment?
options: Tamil Nadu District Planning Committee Act, 1994
Tamil Nadu Town and Country Planning Act, 1971
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu District Planning Committee Act, 1994 was enacted to comply with Article 243ZD of the Constitution.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution of India can the President promulgate an ordinance when both Houses of Parliament are not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances. Article 213 is for the Governor of a state.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President. Which of the following statements regarding this appointment is correct?
options: The President appoints any person as Prime Minister.
The President appoints the leader of the largest party in the Lok Sabha as Prime Minister.
The President appoints a person who is likely to command the confidence of the Lok Sabha.
The President appoints the leader of the majority party in the Rajya Sabha as Prime Minister.
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Prime Minister, and the other ministers on his advice, but the Prime Minister must command a majority in the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: In the context of the Union Council of Ministers, the principle of 'collective responsibility' means:
options: The Cabinet is collectively responsible to the Rajya Sabha.
The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.
The Prime Minister is individually responsible to the President.
The Ministers are collectively responsible to the Prime Minister.
questions.explanation: Article 75(3) states that the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, which gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions, added which new part to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: Part IX, containing Articles 243 to 243-O, was added by the 73rd Amendment. Part IX-A was added for Municipalities by the 74th Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to Indian citizens and not to foreign nationals?
options: Right to equality before the law
Right to freedom of speech and expression
Right to protection in respect of conviction for offences
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, assembly, association, etc.) is guaranteed only to citizens. Other rights like Articles 14, 20, 21, 25 are available to all persons.

questions.question: The 'Doctrine of Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in which landmark case?
options: Shankari Prasad v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills v. Union of India
questions.explanation: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established that Parliament cannot amend the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is based on the Gandhian philosophy?
options: Uniform civil code for the citizens
Promotion of international peace and security
Free and compulsory education for children
questions.explanation: Article 40 (organisation of village panchayats) is a Gandhian principle. Uniform civil code is a Liberal-Intellectual principle.

questions.question: In the election of the President of India, the value of the vote of an MLA is determined by:
options: The total population of the state divided by the total number of elected MLAs
The total population of the state divided by the total number of elected MLAs, further divided by 1000
The total population of the state divided by the total number of elected MLAs, divided by 100
The total number of elected MLAs divided by the total population of the state
questions.explanation: The value of an MLA's vote is calculated as (State Population / Total Elected MLAs) / 1000.

questions.question: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, the term of office for a Panchayat President is:
options: Co-terminus with the term of the Panchayat
questions.explanation: The term of a Panchayat President in Tamil Nadu is five years, co-terminus with the term of the Panchayat.

questions.question: In the context of the Indian Constitution, the executive power of the Union is vested in whom, and is exercised by whom?
options: The President, through officers subordinate to him
The Prime Minister, through the Council of Ministers
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests executive power of the Union in the President, which he exercises either directly or through officers subordinate to him.

questions.question: Who among the following is the ex-officio Chairman of the NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India serves as the ex-officio Chairperson of the NITI Aayog, which replaced the Planning Commission.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The power of judicial review, where courts can strike down laws unconstitutional, is borrowed from the US Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the President of India's election?
questions.explanation: The President of India is elected indirectly by an electoral college, not directly by the people.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, which gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions, added which new part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX, entitled 'The Panchayats', containing Articles 243 to 243-O.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) contained?
questions.explanation: Part IV of the Constitution, containing Directive Principles of State Policy, spans from Article 36 to Article 51.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's 'Varumun Kappom Thittam' (Preventive Health Scheme) primarily aims to:
options: Provide free bus passes to women
questions.explanation: The 'Varumun Kappom Thittam' is a flagship health scheme of the Tamil Nadu government for early screening and prevention of non-communicable diseases.

questions.question: The President's power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment is exercised under which provision of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 72 grants the President the power to grant pardons, etc., in cases of punishment for offences against Union laws, court-martial, and death sentences.

questions.question: Under which article of the Constitution does the President of India have the power to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 grants the President ordinance-making power, subject to parliamentary approval within six weeks of reassembly.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India holds office during the pleasure of the President. This means the Prime Minister can be removed by the President only when:
options: The Prime Minister loses majority support in Lok Sabha
The Rajya Sabha passes a no-confidence motion
questions.explanation: The President can remove the PM only when the PM loses confidence of the Lok Sabha; otherwise, the PM cannot be removed arbitrarily.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the President's power to grant pardon is correct?
options: It is subject to judicial review
It is absolute and cannot be questioned
It can be exercised only after conviction
It applies only to death sentences
questions.explanation: The President's pardoning power under Article 72 is subject to judicial review, though limited to examine whether the power was exercised arbitrarily.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is not a member of which of the following institutions?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is not a member of the Finance Commission; it consists of a chairperson and other members appointed by the President.

questions.question: Under which constitutional provision can the President of India be impeached for violation of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides the procedure for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: The President of India can address either House of Parliament and send messages under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 86 empowers the President to address either House or both Houses and to send messages to them.

questions.question: In the event of the Prime Minister's death, who is the interim Prime Minister until a new leader is chosen?
options: The President appoints a caretaker PM
questions.explanation: There is no constitutional provision; the President appoints a caretaker PM (usually the senior-most minister) until a new leader is elected.

questions.question: Which of the following committees recommended the establishment of the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) as a separate entity?
questions.explanation: The Gopalaswami Ayyangar Committee (1949) recommended the creation of the Prime Minister's Secretariat (now PMO).

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can the President promulgate an ordinance when both Houses of Parliament are not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during the recess of Parliament, subject to constitutional limitations.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the appointment of the Prime Minister by the President?
questions.explanation: Article 75 states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and other ministers on his advice.

questions.question: The words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added to the Preamble by which Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which Article guarantees 'Equality before law' and 'Equal protection of laws'?
questions.explanation: Article 14 ensures equality before the law and equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

questions.question: The Directive Principle under Article 39A directing the State to provide free legal aid was added by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Article 39A to promote equal justice and free legal aid.

questions.question: As per the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, the minimum age for a person to be elected as Chairperson of a Panchayat at any level is:
questions.explanation: Article 243F requires a person to be at least 21 years of age to be qualified for election to a Panchayat.

questions.question: The Chief Election Commissioner of India is removed from office in the same manner and on the same grounds as which other constitutional authority?
questions.explanation: As per Article 324(5), the CEC can be removed only like a Supreme Court judge, i.e., by a special majority in Parliament.

questions.question: What is the minimum period of service as a High Court judge required for appointment as a judge of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 124(3) requires that a person must have been a High Court judge for at least 5 years to be eligible for Supreme Court.

questions.question: When the President of India exercises a 'pocket veto' on a Bill passed by Parliament, it means the President:
options: Refers the Bill to the Supreme Court
Sends the Bill to the State Legislatures
questions.explanation: Pocket veto refers to the President's power to keep a Bill pending without any time limit, effectively withholding assent.

questions.question: To be eligible for election as President of India, a person must have completed which minimum age?
questions.explanation: Article 58(1)(b) requires a candidate for President to be at least 35 years of age.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the President of India elected?
questions.explanation: Article 54 provides for the election of the President by an electoral college consisting of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and state legislative assemblies.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President, and other ministers appointed on the PM's advice.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the impeachment of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 61 lays down the procedure for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: In the event of the death of the President of India, who acts as President until a new President is elected?
questions.explanation: Article 65 provides that the Vice President shall act as President in case of a vacancy caused by death, resignation, or removal.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is the head of which body?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers, which aids and advises the President under Article 74.

questions.question: Under which Article can the President of India promulgate ordinances when both Houses of Parliament are not in session?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances during the recess of Parliament, subject to approval within six weeks of reassembly.

questions.question: The doctrine of 'basic structure' of the Indian Constitution was propounded in which landmark case?
questions.explanation: In the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Supreme Court held that Parliament cannot amend the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Conducting elections is the function of the Election Commission; the President does not oversee electoral processes.

questions.question: When no party secures a clear majority in the Lok Sabha, the President usually appoints the Prime Minister based on:
options: The advice of the outgoing Prime Minister
The leader of the largest party or pre-poll coalition
The recommendation of the Election Commission
questions.explanation: By convention, the President invites the leader of the single largest party or pre-poll alliance to form the government and prove majority.

questions.question: Under which article can the President of India be impeached for violation of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides the procedure for impeachment of the President.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is the head of which body?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Council of Ministers as per Article 74.

questions.question: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to which house of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 75(3) states the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The President can impose President's Rule in a state under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 356 allows the President to assume control of a state's administration.

questions.question: The President of India is elected by which system?
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college using the single transferable vote system.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is the chairperson of which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the ex-officio chairperson of NITI Aayog.

questions.question: Who constitutes the electoral college for the election of the President of India?
options: Elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of State Legislative Assemblies
All members of both Houses of Parliament and all members of State Legislatures
Only elected members of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Elected members of Parliament and members of Legislative Councils
questions.explanation: The electoral college consists of elected MPs of both Houses and elected MLAs of states, including NCT of Delhi and Puducherry.

questions.question: Under Article 61, impeachment of the President can be initiated only if the resolution is signed by at least what fraction of the total members of that House?
questions.explanation: The notice must be signed by at least one-fourth of the total members of the House before the resolution is moved.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution is the Prime Minister appointed by the President?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and other ministers on his advice.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required for a person to be eligible for election as President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 58 of the Constitution prescribes that the President must be at least 35 years of age.

questions.question: Under Article 123, the President can promulgate an ordinance only when?
options: Both Houses of Parliament are not in session
Either House of Parliament is not in session
Lok Sabha is not in session
Rajya Sabha is not in session
questions.explanation: The President can issue an ordinance only when both Houses of Parliament are not in session, as per Article 123(1).

questions.question: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 75(3) states that the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The power of the President to grant pardons under Article 72 extends to which of the following cases?
questions.explanation: Article 72 covers death sentences, court martial cases, and offences against any law within Union executive power.

questions.question: If the Prime Minister resigns, what happens to the Council of Ministers?
options: It continues to function until a new PM is appointed
The Senior Minister acts as interim PM
questions.explanation: The resignation of the Prime Minister leads to the dissolution of the entire Council of Ministers under Article 75(2).

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Vice-President of India is correct?
options: He is the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha
He acts as President when the latter is impeached
He is elected by members of Lok Sabha only
He holds office for a term of six years
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha as per Article 64.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the election of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 54 provides for the election of the President by an electoral college consisting of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and state legislatures.

questions.question: Who among the following was the first President of independent India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the first President of India from 1950 to 1962.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 25 years old to be a member of Lok Sabha, which is a prerequisite for PM.

questions.question: The President of India can issue ordinances under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: President's rule in a state is imposed under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 356 deals with the imposition of President's rule in a state on grounds of failure of constitutional machinery.

questions.question: Which of the following Presidents of India hails from Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: All three were born in present-day Tamil Nadu: Kalam in Rameswaram, Radhakrishnan in Tiruttani, Venkataraman in Rajamadam.

questions.question: The President can be removed from office through impeachment under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides the procedure for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: Under Article 75 of the Indian Constitution, who appoints the Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and other ministers on the advice of the PM.

questions.question: The President of India can be removed from office by impeachment for violation of the Constitution. Which article deals with this?
questions.explanation: Article 61 provides the procedure for impeachment of the President for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: Who acts as the President of India when both the President and Vice President are unavailable?
questions.explanation: Under Article 65, the Chief Justice of India acts as President when both offices are vacant.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is the head of the Council of Ministers. Which article specifies that?
questions.explanation: Article 74(1) states there shall be a Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President.

questions.question: The President of India has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment. Under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 confers the pardoning power on the President of India.

questions.question: In case of a tie in the election of the President of India, which body decides the dispute?
questions.explanation: Article 71 gives the Supreme Court jurisdiction over disputes arising out of Presidential elections.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is the ex-officio chairman of which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the Chairperson of NITI Aayog, which replaced the Planning Commission.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Constitution made the advice of the Council of Ministers binding on the President?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) made the President bound by the advice of the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Under Article 123, the President can promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session. What is the maximum period of an ordinance unless approved by Parliament?
questions.explanation: An ordinance lapses 6 weeks after the reassembly of Parliament unless approved earlier.

questions.question: The Prime Minister of India is appointed by the President. In case no party has a clear majority, who usually gets the first opportunity to form the government?
questions.explanation: Conventionally, the President invites the leader of the single largest party to form the government.

questions.question: Which of the following constitutes the Electoral College for the election of the President of India?
options: All members of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Only elected members of Lok Sabha
Elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of State Legislative Assemblies
Elected members of Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies
questions.explanation: The electoral college consists of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of Legislative Assemblies of States (including Delhi and Puducherry). Nominated members do not vote.

questions.question: With reference to the appointment of the Prime Minister of India, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The Prime Minister must be a member of the Lok Sabha at the time of appointment
The Prime Minister is directly elected by the people
The President appoints the leader of the party with absolute majority in Lok Sabha
The Prime Minister need not be a member of Parliament at the time of appointment but must become one within six months
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Prime Minister who is most likely to command confidence of Lok Sabha; he/she need not be a member at the time but must become a member of either House within six months.

questions.question: The power of the President of India to dissolve the Lok Sabha is exercised:
options: Only on the advice of the Prime Minister
With the consent of the Chief Justice of India
Only after the passage of a no-confidence motion
questions.explanation: The President dissolves Lok Sabha on the advice of the Prime Minister, though in exceptional situations (e.g., no confidence) the President may exercise discretion.

questions.question: Under which of the following circumstances can the President of India promulgate an Ordinance under Article 123 when Parliament is not in session, but the subject matter falls within the State List?
options: A. Never, as Ordinance-making power is limited to Union List
B. Only during President's Rule under Article 356
C. Only with prior consent of the Governor
D. Always, if the Bill would have been valid for Parliament
questions.explanation: The President can legislate on State List subjects only when a Proclamation under Article 356 is in operation, as Parliament then assumes the state's legislative powers.

questions.question: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President, but the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha. Which of the following best describes the President's discretionary power in appointing the Prime Minister?
options: A. No discretion; must appoint the leader of the largest party
B. Discretion only when no party has a clear majority
C. Absolute discretion to appoint any Lok Sabha member
D. Discretion only to appoint a Rajya Sabha member
questions.explanation: The President has discretion only when no single party commands a clear majority; he may then appoint a person who he believes can secure a vote of confidence.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Preamble of the Indian Constitution: 1. It was adopted on 26 November 1949. 2. The term 'Socialist' was added by the 42nd Amendment. 3. It is enforceable in a court of law. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 3 is incorrect; the Preamble is not enforceable in court. Statements 1 and 2 are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens of India and not to foreign nationals?
options: A. Right to equality before law (Article 14)
B. Right to protection of life and personal liberty (Article 21)
C. Right to freedom of speech and expression (Article 19)
questions.explanation: Article 19 (freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) is specifically guaranteed only to citizens, while Articles 14, 21, and 32 are available to all persons.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is specifically aimed at promoting the interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other weaker sections?
options: A. Article 39A (Equal justice and free legal aid)
B. Article 46 (Promotion of educational and economic interests)
C. Article 43 (Living wage, etc., for workers)
D. Article 47 (Duty of State to raise nutrition level)
questions.explanation: Article 46 directs the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of the weaker sections, particularly SCs and STs.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj in Tamil Nadu, which of the following is the correct hierarchical order of rural local bodies?
options: A. Village Panchayat → Panchayat Union → District Panchayat
B. Panchayat Union → Village Panchayat → District Panchayat
C. District Panchayat → Panchayat Union → Village Panchayat
D. Village Panchayat → District Panchayat → Panchayat Union
questions.explanation: The three-tier system in Tamil Nadu is: Village Panchayat at the base, Panchayat Union (Block) at the middle, and District Panchayat at the apex.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a qualification for appointment as a Judge of the Supreme Court of India?
options: A. Has been a Judge of a High Court for at least five years
B. Has been an advocate of a High Court for at least ten years
C. Is, in the opinion of the President, a distinguished jurist
D. Has been a Chief Justice of a High Court for at least three years
questions.explanation: There is no specific requirement of being a Chief Justice of a High Court for three years; the Constitution only mentions being a High Court judge for five years or an advocate for ten years.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is responsible for the conduct of elections to which of the following bodies?
options: A. Parliament and State Legislatures only
B. Parliament, State Legislatures, and Local Bodies
C. Parliament, State Legislatures, and Offices of President and Vice-President
D. Parliament and Offices of President and Vice-President only
questions.explanation: The Election Commission conducts elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President; local body elections are conducted by State Election Commissions.

questions.question: Which of the following Tamil Nadu government policies is directly related to the implementation of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
options: B. Tamil Nadu Solar Energy Policy 2019
C. Tamil Nadu Free Breakfast Scheme for School Children
questions.explanation: The Free Breakfast Scheme aims to fulfill Article 47 (nutrition) and Article 39(e) (health of children), reflecting Directive Principles.

questions.question: If the President of India returns a Bill (other than a Money Bill) for reconsideration to the Parliament, and the Parliament passes it again with or without amendments, the President:
options: A. Must give assent to the Bill
B. May again return the Bill for reconsideration
D. Must consult the Supreme Court before giving assent
questions.explanation: Under Article 111, if a Bill is returned and passed again by Parliament, the President must give his assent; he cannot veto it a second time.

questions.question: Under Article 75 of the Indian Constitution, who is the head of the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Article 75(1) states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and the other Ministers shall be appointed on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the President's power to promulgate ordinances is correct?
options: The President can promulgate an ordinance only when both Houses of Parliament are in session
An ordinance must be approved by Parliament within six weeks of reassembly
The President can promulgate an ordinance on any subject, including those in the State List
An ordinance has the same force as a law but can be challenged on the ground of lack of necessity
questions.explanation: Under Article 123, an ordinance ceases to operate six weeks after Parliament reassembles unless approved, and it can be challenged if the President's satisfaction is malafide or irrelevant.

questions.question: In the context of the Prime Minister's resignation, which of the following is correct?
options: The resignation of the Prime Minister automatically dissolves the Council of Ministers
The President can reject the resignation and ask the Prime Minister to continue
The resignation must be accepted by the President, and a new Prime Minister is appointed
The resignation leads to the dissolution of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Under Article 75(2), the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha; resignation of the PM leads to the fall of the entire ministry unless the President reappoints the same PM.

questions.question: Which of the following is a condition for the President to exercise the power of pardon under Article 72?
options: The pardon can be granted only after a conviction by a court-martial
The President must consult the Prime Minister before granting pardon
The power extends to cases where the sentence is a death sentence
The pardon can be granted only for offences against laws made by the State Legislature
questions.explanation: Article 72(1)(c) specifically includes cases where the sentence is of death, and the power extends to all offences against Union laws, court-martial, and death sentences.

questions.question: Who among the following has the authority to decide whether a bill is a Money Bill under Article 110?
questions.explanation: Article 110(3) states that the Speaker's decision on whether a bill is a Money Bill is final.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the President's role in the appointment of the Prime Minister is correct?
options: The President must appoint the leader of the largest party in Lok Sabha
The President has no discretion if a single party has a clear majority
The President can appoint any person as Prime Minister who is not a member of Parliament
The appointment must be ratified by the Supreme Court within 30 days
questions.explanation: When a single party has a clear majority, the President is bound to appoint its leader; discretion arises only in case of a hung house or coalition.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, the Prime Minister can be a member of which of the following?
options: Either Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha
Any House of Parliament or a State Legislature
questions.explanation: Article 75(5) requires the PM to be a member of either House of Parliament, and if not, they must become one within six months.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the relationship between the President and the Prime Minister in India?
options: The President can dismiss the Prime Minister at any time without any reason
The Prime Minister is the head of state while the President is the head of government
The President exercises executive power on the aid and advice of the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
The Prime Minister is directly elected by the people
questions.explanation: Article 74(1) states that the President shall act on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister, making the PM the de facto executive head.

questions.question: The President can return a bill (other than a Money Bill) for reconsideration under Article 111. What is the effect if the bill is passed again by Parliament?
options: The President can veto the bill by withholding assent
The bill is deemed to have lapsed
The President can refer it to the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: Article 111 states that if a bill is returned and passed again by both Houses, the President shall not withhold assent.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct statement regarding the Prime Minister's role in the appointment of the Chief Election Commissioner?
options: The Prime Minister makes the final decision alone
The President appoints the CEC on the recommendation of the Prime Minister and the Cabinet
The Prime Minister has no role; the President appoints on the advice of the Election Commission
The CEC is appointed by the President after consultation with the Chief Justice of India
questions.explanation: Under Article 324(2), the President appoints the CEC and Election Commissioners, and in practice, the Prime Minister's recommendation is considered, but the Constitution requires consultation with the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the pardoning power of the President (Article 72) and the Governor (Article 161) is correct?
options: President can pardon death sentence, Governor cannot
Governor can pardon sentences of court-martial
President's power is exclusive for offences against union laws
Governor can pardon for offences against state laws only
questions.explanation: Article 72 allows President to pardon death sentences; Article 161 does not empower Governor to pardon death sentences.

questions.question: If a state is under President's Rule (Article 356) and its Legislative Assembly is dissolved, how are the votes of that state cast in the election of the President of India?
options: The Governor of the state casts the votes
The members of the dissolved Assembly continue to vote
The votes of that state are not taken into account
The Chief Minister of the state casts the votes
questions.explanation: Under Article 356, if the Assembly is dissolved, there are no elected MLAs to vote; the state's vote value is not counted.

questions.question: Consider the statements: (1) The Prime Minister must be a member of the Lok Sabha at the time of appointment. (2) A person who is not a member of either House can be appointed as Prime Minister but must become a member within six months. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: A Prime Minister can be from Rajya Sabha, so (1) false. The Constitution requires any non-member appointee to become a member within six months, so (2) true.

questions.question: An ordinance promulgated by the President under Article 123 ceases to operate when:
options: Six weeks elapse after the reassembly of both Houses
The Lok Sabha alone disapproves it
The Rajya Sabha alone disapproves it
Both Houses pass a resolution disapproving it
questions.explanation: Under Article 123(2)(a), an ordinance ceases if both Houses pass resolutions disapproving it; otherwise it lapses after six weeks from reassembly.

questions.question: Which of the following is a constitutionally recognised ground for the impeachment of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 61 specifies 'violation of the Constitution' as the sole ground for impeachment; incompetence and corruption are not mentioned.

questions.question: If the Prime Minister resigns, the Council of Ministers:
options: Continues in office until a new PM is appointed
Can continue if the President approves
questions.explanation: Under Article 75(2), the Council is collectively responsible to Lok Sabha; resignation of PM dissolves the Council as the PM is its head.

questions.question: Which type of bill can the President return to Parliament for reconsideration?
options: Both Constitutional Amendment and Ordinary Bills
questions.explanation: A Money Bill cannot be returned (Article 111 proviso). A Constitutional Amendment Bill is binding on the President. Only Ordinary Bills can be returned.

questions.question: Consider the statements: (1) The President can appoint a Prime Minister who is not a member of the Lok Sabha. (2) The President can dismiss a Prime Minister if the latter loses majority support. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: A Prime Minister may be from Rajya Sabha (1 true). The President cannot dismiss a PM; the PM must resign or be removed by no-confidence vote (2 false).

questions.question: The President's power to consult the Supreme Court under Article 143 is:
options: Binding on both the President and the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: Article 143 gives the President advisory jurisdiction; the Supreme Court's opinion is not binding on the President.

questions.question: Under Article 356, a Proclamation of President's Rule in a state must be approved by Parliament within:
questions.explanation: Article 356(3) requires parliamentary approval within two months; thereafter it can be extended up to six months with approval.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the President's power to grant pardons under Article 72 is correct?
options: It is exclusive and not subject to judicial review.
It can be exercised only after a conviction by a court-martial.
It extends to cases where the sentence is for an offence against a law relating to a matter to which the executive power of the Union extends.
It is available only for death sentences in all cases.
questions.explanation: Article 72 allows the President to grant pardons for offences against Union laws; it is subject to limited judicial review and covers all sentences, not just death.
